stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The coelacanth is a deep-sea fish.
  • Up until 1938, it was believed to have been extinct for about 65 million years.
  • That year, a live coelacanth was caught off the coast of South Africa.
  • The coelacanth is considered a Lazarus species.
  • “Lazarus species” is a term for living species of organisms that were once believed to be extinct.
stem: The student wants to define the term “Lazarus species” and provide an example of one. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals?

answerOptions.content: The coelacanth, a deep-sea fish, was caught off the coast of South Africa in 1938.
Sometimes, a species once believed to be extinct is rediscovered alive in the wild.
One example of a Lazarus species is the coelacanth, a deep-sea fish that was caught off the coast of South Africa in 1938.
The term “Lazarus species” describes a living species of organism, such as the coelacanth, that was once believed to be extinct.

rationale: Choice D is the best answer because it both defines the term "Lazarus species" and names the coelacanth as an example. Choice A is incorrect because it discusses the coelacanth without defining the term or labeling it a Lazarus species. Choice B is incorrect because it describes the concept generally without defining the term or giving an example. Choice C is incorrect because it gives an example without defining the term.
